Add any <strong>Google Font</strong> — i: Add any Google Font — it downloads to this device and renders in the app, your tools and every export. One is always the primary; another can serve as your code face.
Add whichever marks you have — each <str: Add whichever marks you have — each orientation (horizontal, vertical) in each treatment (primary and mono, each with a reverse form for dark backgrounds), plus any marks your brand names its own way — an icon, a crest. A brand with more than one logo can carry each as its own set. Every slot is optional. PNG, SVG, JPEG or WebP; they stay on this device and travel in your brand file.

Every colour your brand carries. Click a: Every colour your brand carries. Click a swatch to recolour, rename or remove it; each section folds and grows with its own + Add. The Colour chart below plots the same swatches by hue and chroma. Changes flow to every picker, tool and export.
Every tool, page and export follows the: Every tool, page and export follows the primary face — headings, body copy and UI alike. Sub-heading, call-to-action and italic roles arrive here as tokens tools can read.
Every tool, page and export now follows: Every tool, page and export now follows it. Where to next?
Finished creative assets from simple inp: Finished creative assets from simple inputs — pick a path, change your mind any time.
Generate matching colours from your prim: Generate matching colours from your primary — pick a harmony, then + Add the ones you want to your brand. Each comes pre-named; rename any of them later. See the whole palette on real graphics below.

Optional colour tokens — blends of your: Optional colour tokens — blends of your palette for backgrounds and accents. Stops wear your swatches, so they follow a recolour. Skip these entirely if your brand doesn't do gradients.

Changes here preview live across the whole app. "Use this colour" re-derives the palette beside — Save colour is what actually keeps it.

The rest of the system — spacing, sizing: The rest of the system — spacing, sizing, stroke widths, opacity, rotation, plain numbers and shadows. Tools that read tokens follow these the way they follow your colours.
This brand is fixed — imports are turned: This brand is fixed — imports are turned off.
This brand is fixed — there is nothing o: This brand is fixed — there is nothing of yours to export.
This build ships with a fixed brand — it: This build ships with a fixed brand — its colours, fonts and tokens are what every tool and export use. Brand adjustment is turned off here, so there’s nothing to change.
This build ships with a fixed brand — its colours, fonts and tokens are what the whole app, your tools and every export wear. Brand editing is turned off here.

What each face is <em>for</em> — the rol: What each face is for — the roles tools and the app read. Headings, body and UI wear the primary; code and data wear the mono face.
